pages.raw_text:
"The national irrigation con-
gress to be held In Spokane in
1909 will bring not less than 10000
visitors to this city," said Secre-
tary l.. G. Monroe in preliminary
report to the chamber of COM
merce at that body's regular
luncheon today. Mr.. Monroe furth-
er stated that Spokane must be-
gin to prepare for this important
occasion Immediately so that 1t
may be handled in a manner cred
table to the city.


The report of Chairman Goodall
of the Fernie relief committee was
read and showed that more than
$4,000 was spent for the rlief of
the Fernie sufferers within 10 days
after word was first received of
that city's plight.


Il. Il. G. Van Dissel, on behalf
Of the apple show, stated that
preparations for its successful
are moving smooth
ly but that the committee will still
need between $4,000 and $4,000 and
eXhorted the chamber to bend ev-
cry effort to assist In raising this
amount within the next seven
weeks.


Letters from various parts of the
United States were read by Sec
rotary Monroe congratulating Spo-
kane on getting the irrigation con
Sress for 1909.
